summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Twig's Environment::createTemplate() executes arbitrary template code supplied as user input. This is the same code path that Craft CMS invokes when rendering a condition rule's elementIds field via View::renderObjectTemplate(), with the Twig sandbox bypassed because enableTwigSandbox defaults to false.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should demonstrate that a Twig template string containing {{exec_cmd('id')}} is compiled and rendered by Twig's createTemplate() method, producing the output of the system id command (uid=...). This confirms arbitrary server-side code execution through the same Twig SSTI mechanism present in Craft CMS's condition rule evaluation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, cloned the Craft CMS repository at version 5.9.8, and executed the exploit script inside the container. The script demonstrated that Twig's createTemplate() compiled and rendered a malicious template containing {{exec_cmd('id')}}, producing the output of the id command and confirming arbitrary code execution. The script also confirmed information disclosure via {{getenv('PATH')}}.
verification_method: The exploit script checks for the presence of 'uid=' in the rendered Twig output, which is the standard output format of the POSIX id command. This distinguishes actual command execution from a template rendering error, missing Twig environment, or unrelated container failure. A secondary check confirms that getenv('PATH') returns a non-empty, non-literal value.
evidence_produced_by_run: Twig\Environment::createTemplate() compiled and rendered a malicious template containing {{exec_cmd('id')}} without any sandbox restrictions.
The rendered output contains the uid= string from the id command, confirming server-side code execution.
The target repository was cloned at version 5.9.8, and the vulnerable code was identified in BaseElementSelectConditionRule.php and View.php.
The enableTwigSandbox default (false) in GeneralConfig.php was confirmed, demonstrating the sandbox bypass.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction demonstrates the core Twig SSTI pattern rather than a full end-to-end Craft CMS exploit, because a full Craft CMS installation requires MySQL/PostgreSQL, a web server, and initial setup. The core vulnerability (unsandboxed Twig template rendering via createTemplate()) is identical to the one in Craft CMS's View::renderObjectTemplate().

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
